medical_history.chief_complaint: redness and pain in the right eye and a headache
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: At the age of 36, she had undergone surgery for bilateral cataracts. Menopause had occurred at the age of 40. There was no history of consanguinity. Her husband (60 years old) was well and she had a son (26 years old) and daughter (29 years old) who were delivered normally and were healthy. She was independent in daily activities.
medical_history.past_medical_history: bilateral cataracts, menopause at the age of 40

physical_examination.general_examination: appearance of a 70-year-old woman, was 146 cm high and weighed 36 kg. She had a "bird-like" face with a hawk nose and a small jaw and poliosis of the hair, eyebrows, and eyelashes. Her voice was high-pitched and hoarse, and her skin was dry and atrophic, as well as being lustrous and pigmented, with scleroderma-like changes. There was atrophy of the muscles and adipose tissue of the extremities and plain radiography revealed calcification of both Achilles’ tendons.
